findings.summary: Legacy segment/travel migration paths are reduced but still not fully retired
evidence: Deprecated markers and compatibility scaffolding remain concentrated in travel-between-images and segment settings pathways.
Runtime paths still include migration adapters beyond boundary-only translation points.
findings.suggestion: Schedule one migration-closure pass to collapse remaining adapters into a single edge shim and remove legacy branches from active business-flow modules.
findings.reasoning: Finishing the migration tail removes hidden branch complexity and improves long-term contract clarity.
